title: Self-Criticism Awareness Log
description: Track self-critical thoughts as they happen. Logging these moments helps you notice patterns, identify triggers, and practice responding with greater self-compassion over time.
instructions: When you notice a self-critical thought, pause and complete this log as soon as you can. The goal is not to stop all self-criticism immediately — it is to build awareness and practice choosing a kinder response.

fields.label: The self-critical thought or message
fields.placeholder: Write the exact words of the inner critic, e.g., 'You're so stupid — you always mess things up'

fields.label: What triggered the self-criticism?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Made a mistake at work, saw someone else's success on social media, received feedback

fields.label: How intense was the self-criticism? (0 = barely noticeable, 10 = overwhelming)
How did you respond to the self-criticism?
A kinder or more balanced perspective
fields.placeholder: e.g., 'Everyone makes mistakes — this one doesn't define me', 'I'm learning and that takes time'

fields.label: Any other notes or observations
fields.placeholder: e.g., I noticed the self-criticism was loudest when I was tired

disclaimer: This log is an educational self-tracking tool. If self-criticism is persistent and significantly affecting your well-being, consider speaking with a mental health professional. If you are in crisis, call or text 988.
